evidence_quotes.quote: A game that is offering a product plus an online service should state that you are paying for both and if one fails to deliver it should be refunded.
It should also be forbiden to create fear of missing out items.
games should display the amount in euros for every microtransaction inside the game.

They should be enforced to ensure that the products can then be transfered in an offline state or even in the hands of volunteers to keep it in a playable state or be forced to refund a part or whole transaction of the product/service.

paragraphs.text: I would kindly request for you to try to enforce companies to be accountable for their products as in gaming products like games. When trying to sell a game and online services and charge a sum of money, I understand that they are not required to upkeep an online server forever so the game is playable in that regard. However they should be enforced to ensure that the products can then be transfered in an offline state or even in the hands of volunteers to keep it in a playable state or be forced to refund a part or whole transaction of the product/service. A game that is offering a product plus an online service should state that you are paying for both and if one fails to deliver it should be refunded. Maybe a way to enforce it is with an offline product + server/online "pass"/fee. They already exist in the realm of gaming. If the online service makes the game unplayable it should be enforced to refund that amount of service that the consumer paid. It should not be mandatory to connect to a server to play a single player game with no matchmaking/online co-op mode. It should be able to be played like a physical copy of game like it used to be. I can still play my Myst game in my computer because i bought the product and have the product at my possesion. Online games should be able to be the same. I should have the files of the game in my computer and to modify or do anything I want, as I could with my physical copy. Nintendo should not be able to render useless a console in their own terms. That would create a massive amount of e-waste. If I buy the console I should be able to modify is as I see fit. I should be able to emulate my own games in any media I see fit. I can see my pictures either in my pc or my TV or I can print them. I should be able to play my games in my pc or cast them in another hardware if I wanted to. In another subject, games should be enforced to not have in game currencies and should display the amount in euros for every microtransaction inside the game. It should not create uneven different currencies in order to trick the consumer to believe that an amount is small or create difficult exchange rates to make it harder to keep track of one's expenses. It should also display the chances of getting an item. The random formula in mystery boxes like microtransactions should inform the consumer the chances of getting an item. It should also be enforced to be forbiden to create fear of missing out items. Digital cosmetic and other products do not have a limited supply. It should not be removed, it should not be seasonal, there should not be a pop up saying "In shop for 3 days only!". Seasonal pass should keep every achievement and item bought or acquired from the previous seasonal passes.
